TypeSignature.Value: .class public sequential ansi sealed beforefieldinit SKFourByteTag extends System.ValueType implements class System.IEquatable`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag>

Docs.summary: Represents a four-byte tag (FourCC) used to identify OpenType font tables, variation axes, and other resources.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
`SKFourByteTag` represents a four-character code (FourCC) packed into a 32-bit unsigned integer, where the first character occupies the most-significant byte. This format is used throughout OpenType and Skia for identifying font tables, variation axes, and other tagged resources.
You can construct an `SKFourByteTag` from four characters (e.g., `new SKFourByteTag('w','g','h','t')`), from a raw `uint`, or by parsing a string with `SKFourByteTag.Parse`. The implicit conversions to and from `uint` make it interoperable with raw tag values.
This struct is a value type and does not require disposal.
## Examples
```csharp var wght = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wght"); var ital = new SKFourByteTag('i', 't', 'a', 'l'); Console.WriteLine(wght.ToString()); // "wght" ```

MemberSignature.Value: public SKFourByteTag (uint value);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(unsigned int32 value) cil managed

Docs.param: The raw 32-bit unsigned integer value of the tag.

MemberSignature.Value: public static SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag Parse (string? tag);
.method public static hidebysig valuetype SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag Parse(string tag) cil managed

Docs.param: The string to parse. Only the first four characters are used; shorter strings are padded with spaces.
Docs.returns: An parsed from the string. If is or empty, returns a tag with the value zero. Strings shorter than four characters are padded with spaces; longer strings are truncated.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string ToString ();
.method public hidebysig virtual instance string ToString() cil managed

Docs.summary: Returns the four-character string representation of this tag.
Docs.returns: A four-character string representation of the tag.
